name: List Data Structures 2
description: Test your knowledge on linked lists, circular lists, heterogeneous lists, and double-ended queues.

questions.question: What distinguishes a doubly linked list from a singly linked list?
options: Each node contains two data fields instead of one.
Nodes are allocated in a contiguous block of memory.
Each node stores pointers to both the next and previous nodes.
It can only be traversed from the tail to the head node.
questions.explain: Nodes in a doubly linked list maintain pointers to both the next and the previous elements, allowing bidirectional traversal.

questions.question: In a standard circular singly linked list, where does the pointer of the last node point to?
options: The head node of the list
A null or empty reference
The middle node of the list
Back to the last node itself
questions.explain: In a circular linked list, the last node's next pointer links back to the first node, forming a continuous loop.

questions.question: What is a major advantage of using a doubly linked list over a singly linked list?
options: It consumes significantly less memory space per node.
It allows for efficient node deletion when given only the target node pointer.
It provides faster random access to items via their integer index.
It guarantees that data elements remain ordered automatically.
questions.explain: Since a node in a doubly linked list knows its predecessor, it can be unlinked in O(1) time without traversing from the head.

questions.question: Which term best describes a list data structure that can store elements of completely different data types concurrently?
options: Homogeneous list
Heterogeneous list
Dynamic array list
Circular array list
questions.explain: A heterogeneous list is designed to hold objects or values of diverse data types within the same collection.

questions.question: What is a primary characteristic of a double-ended queue (Deque)?
options: Elements are strictly restricted to insertion at the front and removal from the rear.
Elements can only be inserted or removed from the exact middle of the structure.
Elements can be efficiently inserted and removed from both the front and the rear.
Elements are automatically sorted in ascending order upon every new insertion.
questions.explain: A deque permits insertion and deletion operations at both ends, combining features of stacks and queues.

questions.question: Which operation is typically inefficient in a standard singly linked list compared to an array?
options: Inserting an element right at the beginning
Deleting the first node of the structure
Accessing an element at an arbitrary index
Traversing through the entire collection
questions.explain: Arrays provide O(1) random access via indexing, whereas a singly linked list requires O(n) sequential traversal to reach a specific index.

questions.question: In a circular doubly linked list, what does the 'previous' pointer of the head node point to?
options: The tail node of the list
A null pointer reference
The head node itself
The second node in line
questions.explain: To complete the circular structure, the head node's previous pointer links directly to the final node of the collection.

questions.question: How are heterogeneous lists typically implemented in strongly-typed object-oriented programming languages?
options: By enforcing fixed primitive allocations inside uniform arrays.
By storing pointers or references to a common base class or interface.
By duplicating the entire underlying list structure for every data type.
By completely avoiding the use of pointers or dynamic memory references.
questions.explain: Using polymorphism with a base class (like Object) or an interface allows a list to store diverse object types references uniformly.

questions.question: What is a common real-world application for a circular linked list structure?
options: Implementing a deep undo/redo mechanism in applications
Managing active execution time slots in round-robin CPU scheduling
Parsing complex algebraic formulas using operator precedence
Storing key-value pairs inside a standard hash map bucket
questions.explain: Circular linked lists naturally model cyclical processes, making them perfect for round-robin scheduling algorithms.

questions.question: Which of the following data structures can be used directly as an efficient implementation provider for both a Stack and a Queue?
options: Singly Linked List
Heterogeneous List
Double-ended Queue
Static Matrix List
questions.explain: Because a double-ended queue supports additions and removals at both ends, it can easily replicate stack (LIFO) or queue (FIFO) behaviors.
